title: Largest Element in Array
description:
Given an array of integers `nums`, return the largest element in the array.

You must solve the problem using an algorithm that runs in `O(n log n)` time complexity or better.

examples.explanation: The largest element in the array [2, 7, 11, 15] is 15.
The largest element in the array [3, 2, 4] is 4.

driverCode.python:
import sys
from typing import List

if __name__ == '__main__':
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split('\n')
    if len(input_data) >= 2:
        nums = list(map(int, input_data[1].split()))
        sol = Solution()
        print(sol.findLargest(nums))

hints: Use a sorting algorithm to sort the array and return the last element.
Alternatively, you can iterate through the array to find the maximum element.

solution.approach: You can either iterate through the array keeping track of the maximum value seen so far, or sort the array and pick the last element.
